steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage traces Barragan's shift from historically inspired early houses to modernist designs he developed after international travels in the 1930s. The correct answer must reflect that cause-and-effect: travels abroad triggered the stylistic change.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '"paragons" framing': Labels two specific projects as paragons of his career rather than showing a shift across it. Overstates and misrepresents the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — 'invented contrast': Implies a distinction between project types that the passage never draws. ✗
  • Choice C — 'style reversal': Says Barragan reversed a stylistic choice, but the passage describes a forward evolution, not a reversal. ✗
  • Choice D — 'shift tied to time abroad': Directly links Barragan's adoption of functionalism and modernism to his international travels, matching the passage's own cause-effect structure. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
